sourceMovie.title: The Wiz
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Wiz (1978) as a music theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The music and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wizard of Oz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wiz clicked for you, The Wizard of Oz (1939) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Anastasia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wiz clicked for you, Anastasia (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Conan the Barbarian

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wiz?
faq.answer: Try The Wizard of Oz, Anastasia, Conan the Barbarian—each shares DNA with The Wiz while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wiz?
faq.answer: The Wiz (1978) is best described as Music, Fantasy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wiz worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this music lane.
